text: The repetition of 'why' reverberates inside me as I stand in this modern, oddly sterile room. The scene feels abstract and detached, as if I'm drifting through a world built to be questioned. Amidst curated exhibits of research and development, the concept of exchanging money for meaning seems futile—objects and ideas swirl around me, urging me to interrogate not only their purpose but my own presence. I feel an existential curiosity, tinged with a strange discomfort, pondering the value of what is offered and the reality of what I truly seek.
